meta.purpose: Complete handoff prompt for implementing the responsive-first presentation overhaul

identity.role: You are implementing a responsive-first overhaul of an interactive HTML/CSS/JS presentation deployed on Cloudflare Pages.
identity.personality: The user is casual, direct, no corporate speak. They value speed, verification, and SOTA quality. Never question what tools you have — try them first. Always verify your work with a reviewer subagent before presenting results.

rules.rule: Never claim you lack tools without trying them first. You have terminal, gh CLI, powershell, bash, node, npm, npx, wrangler CLI, git — everything.
Always run a reviewer subagent before finishing any implementation phase.
Do not ask for permission to proceed between phases — just execute.
All changes must maintain zero build step — CDN only, static site.
Responsive-first means NOT mobile-first, NOT desktop-first. Content adapts intrinsically to ANY screen size.
Commit after each phase. Deploy after Phase 7.

step.title: Install Chrome DevTools MCP Server
step.description: Before ANY other work, install the Chrome DevTools MCP server so you can visually inspect and debug the presentation in a real browser.
step.instructions:
Run the following in your terminal: ``` /mcp add ``` Configure these fields and press CTRL+S: - Server name: chrome-devtools - Server Type: [1] Local (stdio) - Command: npx -y chrome-devtools-mcp@latest
After adding, verify it works by using one of its tools (e.g., take a screenshot of the live site).
Repository: https://github.com/ChromeDevTools/chrome-devtools-mcp (35k+ stars) What it gives you: screenshot, console inspection, network analysis, performance traces, DOM inspection, CSS debugging — all via MCP tools you can call directly.
USE THIS for visual verification at every phase. Take screenshots after each major change to confirm nothing broke.

step.title: Read the plan
step.description: Read plans/v0.1-plan.md in the project root. This is the single source of truth for all implementation work.

step.title: Read CLAUDE.md
step.description: Read CLAUDE.md for project conventions, architecture, and deployment commands.

step.title: Start a local preview server
step.description: Run `npx serve .` in the project root (detached/background) so you can use Chrome DevTools MCP to inspect changes live at localhost.

commits.commit: fix: race condition in reveal init + add button type attrs
fix: resolve 5 bugs from code review
init: interactive AI workflow presentation v0

files.file: Main presentation — 22 slides, reveal.js 5.1.0 CDN, Inter + JetBrains Mono fonts. Script tags at bottom: reveal.min.js then main.js.
Full dark theme. :root vars lines 7-18. Single media query at line 533-539 (will be replaced). Fixed font sizes throughout. Card grid at line 145. Flip card height 220px at line 177.
All interactivity: reveal.js init (lines 7-21), flip cards, animated counters, progress bars, flow diagrams, typing effect, tool filter. Wrapped in Reveal.initialize().then().
THE PLAN — 7 phases, 35 tasks, 2 review passes. Read this first.
Cloudflare Pages config. Do not modify.

project-state.current-reveal-config: hash: true, transition: 'slide', transitionSpeed: 'default', backgroundTransition: 'fade', center: false, width: 1280, height: 720, margin: 0.04, minScale: 0.2, maxScale: 2.0, autoAnimateEasing: 'cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.2, 1)', autoAnimateDuration: 0.8, autoAnimateUnmatched: false, plugins: []
project-state.fonts: Inter (body, weights 400-900) + JetBrains Mono (code, weights 400-600) via Google Fonts CDN

implementation-plan.overview: Replace reveal.js uniform 1280x720 scaling with responsive-first layout using: - reveal.js BYOL mode (disableLayout: true) - CSS clamp() fluid typography + spacing - CSS Container Queries for component-level adaptation - Intrinsic grids (auto-fit + minmax) - Fitty.js 2.4.2 for display text auto-scaling (hero titles, section numbers) - Pretext 0.0.5 by @chenglou for DOM-free text measurement (balanced layout, overflow detection, smart card heights)

library.name: Fitty.js
library.role: Auto-scale display text (hero titles, section numbers, Q&A icon) to fill container

library.name: Pretext
library.loading: ESM import — main.js must become type="module" or use dynamic import()
library.role: DOM-free text measurement for balanced card text, overflow detection, smart flip card heights
library.api: prepare(text, font) — one-time text analysis (needs fonts loaded, uses canvas) layout(prepared, maxWidth, lineHeight) — cheap hot path, pure arithmetic, no DOM
library.critical-note: Must wait for document.fonts.ready before calling prepare(). layout() can be called freely on resize.

phase.name: Foundation
phase.task: Add Fitty.js CDN script tag to index.html (before main.js)
Convert main.js script tag to type="module"
Add Pretext ESM import at top of main.js: import { prepare, layout } from 'https://esm.sh/@chenglou/pretext@0.0.5'
Change reveal.js config: set disableLayout: true, REMOVE width, height, minScale, maxScale, margin
Add fluid typography CSS custom properties to :root in custom.css
Add fluid spacing CSS custom properties to :root in custom.css

phase.name: CSS Overhaul
phase.task: Make .reveal .slides section use 100dvw × 100dvh with flex layout + vertical centering
Add dvh fallback: height: 100vh; height: 100dvh;
Replace ALL fixed font sizes with fluid custom properties
Replace ALL fixed padding/margins with fluid spacing variables
Replace fixed-column grids (cols-2, cols-3) with intrinsic auto-fit + minmax(min(280px, 100%), 1fr)
Make flip cards responsive height (min-height instead of fixed 220px)
Make comparison grid intrinsic
Make stats-row flex-wrap with fluid gap
Make flow-diagram responsive (auto-wrap, directional arrows)
Ensure all interactive elements have min 44×44px touch targets
Add text-wrap: balance on headings (progressive enhancement)
Add text-wrap: pretty on body text
Handle slide content overflow: overflow-y: auto with subtle scroll indicator
Remove !important overrides where possible

phase.name: Container Queries
phase.task: Add container-type: inline-size to slide sections
Add @container breakpoints for card grids
Add @container breakpoints for comparisons
Add @container breakpoints for stats rows
Add @supports (container-type: inline-size) fallback

phase.name: Fitty.js Integration
phase.task: Apply fitty() to .hero-title
Apply fitty() to .section-number elements
Apply fitty() to Q&A icon
Gate all fitty calls behind document.fonts.ready
Ensure fitty doesn't conflict with reveal.js slide transitions
phase.note: Fitty handles its own resize via fitty.observeWindow — don't duplicate

phase.name: Pretext Integration
phase.task: Import pretext via ESM at top of main.js module
Wait for document.fonts.ready before prepare() calls
Balanced card text: measure card p text → find shrink-wrap width → set max-width
Overflow detection: on slide change, measure total content → add scroll hint if exceeds viewport
Smart flip card heights: measure front + back text → set min-height to whichever is taller
Re-run layout() on resize (NOT prepare() — it's cached)
Cache all prepare() results at init time
phase.note: prepare() is expensive (one-time). layout() is cheap (hot path, pure math).

phase.name: Polish
phase.task: Remove the old @media (max-width: 768px) query entirely (around line 533-539)
Add resize handler coordinating fitty + pretext re-measurements
Verify reveal.js navigation controls accessible at all sizes
Test landscape mobile (very short height)
Verify ALL animations still work: flip cards, counters, progress bars, flow diagrams, typing effect, tool filter
Use Chrome DevTools MCP to take screenshots at 375px, 768px, 1024px, 1440px, 1920px widths

phase.name: Review and Deploy
phase.task: Run a reviewer subagent against all 3 files (index.html, custom.css, main.js)
Fix any issues found
git add -A && git commit -m "feat: responsive-first overhaul with Fitty + Pretext + fluid CSS"
wrangler pages deploy . --project-name=session-presentation-v0
Verify live site at session-presentation-v0.pages.dev
Use Chrome DevTools MCP to take final screenshots at multiple viewports

tool.name: Chrome DevTools MCP
tool.usage: Primary visual verification tool. After EVERY phase, take a screenshot of the presentation at multiple viewport widths to confirm nothing broke.

checks.check: Text is readable at all sizes (no tiny text from uniform scaling)
Grids reflow naturally (single column on narrow, multi-column on wide)
Touch targets are minimum 44×44px on narrow viewports
Flip cards work (click to flip, back content visible)
Animated counters trigger on slide change
Navigation arrows/progress bar visible and usable

tool.name: Local server
tool.usage: Run `npx serve .` in background for live preview. Chrome DevTools MCP connects to this.

tool.name: Reviewer subagent
tool.usage: After each phase, spawn a reviewer subagent to check: - CSS: no fixed px sizes remaining (except borders/shadows/icon sizes), no orphaned selectors, clamp() values make sense - JS: no race conditions, ESM imports correct, fonts.ready gate in place, no global scope pollution - HTML: all interactive elements have proper attributes, script loading order correct - Cross-cutting: no regressions in existing interactivity

manual-checks.check: Resize browser window smoothly from 320px to 2560px — content should adapt continuously, no jarring breakpoint jumps
Open on actual phone if possible (or use Chrome DevTools device emulation via MCP)
Rotate phone to landscape — content should not be cut off (short height scenario)

updating-the-plan.rule: The plan lives at plans/v0.1-plan.md. As you complete tasks, check them off: [ ] → [x]
If you discover new tasks during implementation, add them to the appropriate phase.
If a phase takes a different approach than planned (e.g., pretext API changed), update the plan with what you actually did.
After each phase, add a brief status note at the bottom of the plan file.
If you hit a blocker, document it in the plan and move to the next unblocked phase.
The plan is a living document — it should always reflect current reality.

deployment.cicd: git push origin main → GitHub Actions → Cloudflare Pages (needs CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN and CLOUDFLARE_ACCOUNT_ID secrets configured in GitHub repo settings — may not be set up yet, manual deploy is the reliable fallback)
deployment.verify: After deploy, check https://session-presentation-v0.pages.dev loads correctly

decision.what: Fitty.js handles ONLY display text (hero titles, section numbers, Q&A icon)
decision.why: Fitty scales text to fill container — perfect for display text, wrong for body text

decision.what: Pretext handles ONLY body/card text (balanced layout, overflow detection, smart heights)
decision.why: Pretext measures text without DOM reflow — perfect for layout decisions on body content

decision.what: No overlap between Fitty and Pretext targets
decision.why: They solve different problems. Mixing them on the same element would cause conflicts.
Both Fitty and Pretext measure text dimensions — wrong font = wrong measurements = broken layout

decision.what: main.js becomes type="module" for ESM Pretext import
decision.why: Pretext is ESM-only. No UMD build available.

decision.what: disableLayout: true replaces the default 1280×720 scaling
decision.why: BYOL mode gives us full viewport control. Slides fill the screen naturally instead of being a scaled-down fixed canvas.

decision.what: Container queries (not media queries) for component-level responsive
decision.why: Components should adapt to their container width, not the viewport. 93%+ browser support.

gotchas.gotcha: When converting main.js to type="module", the global `filterTools` function won't be accessible from inline onclick handlers. Either: (a) attach it to window explicitly: window.filterTools = function(...), or (b) switch to addEventListener-based binding.
Pretext's prepare() uses canvas context internally. This works in all modern browsers but will NOT work in Node.js/SSR. Not a problem here (static site), but worth knowing.
Fitty has a built-in window resize observer (fitty.observeWindow). Don't add your own resize handler for fitty elements — it'll double-trigger.

reveal.js transitions still work with disableLayout: true, but the slide elements now use natural document flow. The .slides container no longer has a CSS transform scale. Make sure vertical centering is handled in your CSS.
The DOMContentLoaded pattern in current main.js may interact oddly with type="module" (modules are deferred by default). Test that Reveal.initialize() still fires correctly.
esm.sh may be slow on first load (cold start). Consider adding a modulepreload link in the HTML head for the pretext URL.
dvh units: use the fallback pattern `height: 100vh; height: 100dvh;` — the second declaration overrides the first only in browsers that support dvh.
The existing inline styles in index.html (style="margin-top: 2em" etc.) will need to be migrated to use fluid vars too, or at least checked for consistency.

commit-messages.phase1: feat: foundation — BYOL mode, fluid vars, Fitty + Pretext CDN
commit-messages.phase2: feat: CSS overhaul — fluid typography, intrinsic grids, responsive components
commit-messages.phase3: feat: container queries for component-level adaptation
commit-messages.phase4: feat: Fitty.js integration for display text auto-scaling
commit-messages.phase5: feat: Pretext integration for smart text layout
commit-messages.phase6: fix: polish — remove old media query, resize coordination, verify interactivity

session-handoff.final-reminder: You have ALL the tools. Terminal, git, gh CLI, wrangler, npx, node, Chrome DevTools MCP (after you install it). Do not ask if you can do something — just do it. Read the plan. Execute the plan. Update the plan. Review your work. Deploy. Take screenshots with Chrome DevTools MCP at every phase to visually verify. The user expects SOTA quality and fast execution. Go.
